meta.name: Ají de Gallina
meta.subtitle: Creamy Peruvian chicken stew with ají amarillo, walnuts, and bread-thickened sauce

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalogs
notes: SSA canonical home method using poached chicken + ají amarillo cream sauce thickened with bread (or crackers) and nuts.
Designed for planner adaptation: canned evaporated milk vs half-and-half, walnut vs pecan, gluten-free thickener swap.

summary: Classic Peruvian comfort dish: shredded chicken in a creamy ají amarillo sauce thickened with soaked bread and walnuts, served over rice with potatoes and egg.

yield.notes: Serves 6 with rice + sides. Sauce thickens as it cools—thin with stock if needed.

signals.weeknightPossible: Possible if using rotisserie chicken and pre-made ají amarillo paste.
signals.freezerFriendly: Sauce can be frozen; texture may slightly change but reheats well with stirring.

heat.note: Ají amarillo is fruity and medium; adjust paste amount based on brand heat.

optional.label: Rice cooker
optional.notes: Helpful to cook rice while sauce simmers.

notes: Contains dairy (milk/cream + optional cheese), gluten (bread) and tree nuts (walnuts).
Serve often includes hard-boiled eggs. Adaptations included below.

ingredients.label: Chicken thighs (bone-in, skin-on) or mixed pieces
ingredients.notes: You can substitute 4–5 cups shredded rotisserie chicken to save time.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Season in layers; adjust at the end.

ingredients.label: Ají amarillo paste
ingredients.notes: Start at 2 tbsp if your paste is very hot; increase to 4 tbsp for a stronger ají flavor.

ingredients.label: White bread, torn (crusts optional)
ingredients.notes: Traditional thickener. Substitute saltines or cooked potato for gluten-free thickening.

ingredients.label: Evaporated milk
ingredients.notes: Classic shortcut. Substitute half-and-half or whole milk + a splash of cream.

ingredients.label: Parmesan (finely grated)
ingredients.notes: Optional but common in many home versions; adds savoriness.

ingredients.label: Reserved chicken stock (from poaching)
ingredients.notes: Use to blend sauce and thin to desired consistency.

ingredients.label: Potatoes, peeled and sliced (for serving)
ingredients.notes: Usually served with sliced boiled potatoes.

ingredients.label: Eggs, hard-boiled (for serving)
ingredients.notes: Typically halved or quartered on top.

steps.title: Poach the chicken and reserve stock
instructions: Add chicken to a pot and cover with water. Add 1 tsp salt, pepper, and bay leaf (optional).
Bring to a gentle simmer (not a hard boil). Cook until chicken is cooked through and tender.
Remove chicken to a plate. Reserve at least 2 cups of the poaching liquid as stock.
When cool enough to handle, remove skin and bones (if used) and shred chicken into bite-size strands.
checks: Chicken reaches 165°F / 74°C in the thickest part.

steps.title: Cook rice, potatoes, and eggs (optional but classic)
instructions: While chicken cooks or cools, cook rice per your usual method.
Boil sliced potatoes in salted water until tender; drain and hold warm.
Hard-boil eggs (optional), cool, peel, and halve or quarter.
Set out olives for garnish.
checks: Potatoes are tender but not falling apart.
Egg yolks are set to preference.

steps.title: Sweat onion and garlic
instructions: Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add chopped onion with a pinch of salt; cook until very soft and translucent (don’t brown heavily).
Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds until fragrant.
checks: Onion is very soft and sweet-smelling.

steps.title: Blend the sauce base (bread + walnuts + milk + ají)
instructions: In a blender, add torn bread, walnuts, evaporated milk, ají amarillo paste, and about 1 cup reserved stock.
Add the cooked onion-garlic mixture.
Blend until very smooth and creamy. If too thick to blend, add a splash more stock.
checks: Mixture is smooth (no visible bread or nut chunks).
Consistency is pourable but thick (like heavy cream to thin batter).

steps.title: Simmer the sauce, then add shredded chicken
instructions: Pour blended sauce into a saucepan over medium-low heat.
Simmer gently, stirring often, until it thickens and loses any raw bread flavor.
Taste and season with remaining salt as needed. Add pepper to taste.
Stir in shredded chicken and simmer 3–5 minutes to warm through.
If using Parmesan, stir in off heat or at very low heat to avoid splitting; thin with stock if needed.
checks: Sauce is velvety and coats a spoon.
Chicken is warmed through and evenly coated.
Flavor balance: creamy + ají fruitiness + savory.

steps.title: Serve Ají de Gallina
instructions: Plate with a mound of rice and sliced boiled potatoes.
Spoon ají de gallina over rice and/or alongside potatoes.
Garnish with egg and black olives. Sprinkle cilantro or parsley if desired.

refrigerate.notes: Cool quickly and store in an airtight container. Sauce thickens in the fridge.

freeze.notes: Freeze sauce + chicken together. Thaw overnight and reheat gently with stock to loosen.

reheat.notes: Reheat gently over low heat, stirring often. Add reserved stock or water to thin to serving consistency.

leftoverUses: Serve over quinoa instead of rice
Stuff into empanadas or turnovers (thicker sauce)
Use as a creamy topping for roasted potatoes
makeAheadNotes: Sauce base can be blended a day ahead and simmered before serving.
Chicken can be poached/shredded ahead; store with a splash of stock to keep moist.

notes: If your ají amarillo paste is very acidic or salty, reduce vinegar-like sharpness by adding a touch more milk and a pinch of sugar.
If sauce tastes flat, add a little more salt and a small squeeze of lime at serving (optional).
Avoid boiling the finished sauce aggressively—gentle heat keeps it smooth and prevents dairy separation.
Use boxed low-sodium chicken stock for blending/thinning.
Skip poaching step; proceed to sauce steps.
Replace bread with 6–8 gluten-free crackers (or 1 cup cooked potato).
Blend as usual; simmer to thicken.
Omit walnuts and add 2 additional slices bread (or extra crackers) for thickness.
Flavor will be less nutty; add a bit more Parmesan (optional) for depth.
Use whole milk instead of evaporated milk; add 1 tbsp olive oil for richness.
Simmer gently; expect slightly thinner sauce.
Poach chicken to 165°F / 74°C and cool promptly before shredding.
Reheat leftovers to steaming hot; avoid prolonged room-temperature holding.
